title: PROJECT KNOWLEDGE BASE
text: R package scRNA-seq experiment design. Guides investigators designing single-cell RNA sequencing experiments shifting focus UMI matrix back raw FastQ reads. Includes C tool fastF pseudo-design dataset generation.

title: PROJECT KNOWLEDGE BASE
code: FastQDesign/ ├── R/              # R package functions (8 files) ├── src/            # C source for fastF tool (11 files) ├── tests/          # testthat tests ├── data/           # package data ├── man/            # R documentation ├── CMakeLists.txt  # fastF build config └── NAMESPACE       # 16 exported functions

title: External Data Sources
text: Download : https://zenodo.org/records/19073177 Contains Cellranger-aligned BAM files cell barcodes (CB tag) UMI (UB tag):

title: External Data Sources
text: Download : https://zenodo.org/records/19072084 Contains pre-processed Seurat objects:

title: External Data Sources
text: BAM files can accessed directly Zenodo URL. Note: Zenodo BAM files large (13-14 GB ), streaming may slow.

title: External Data Sources
code: library(Rsamtools)  bam_url <- "https://zenodo.org/records/19073177/files/P1_AI_possorted_genome_bam.bam" bai_url <- "https://zenodo.org/records/19073177/files/P1_AI_possorted_genome_bam.bam.bai"  bf <- BamFile(bam_url, index = bai_url) header <- scanBamHeader(bf)

title: External Data Sources
text: fastF supports URL streaming downloading recommended large BAM files: Recommendation: large BAM files, download first rather stream.
code: # Works but slow for 13GB files FastQDesign::fastF_crb(   bam = "https://zenodo.org/records/19073177/files/P1_AI_possorted_genome_bam.bam",   out = "output.tsv.gz" )

title: External Data Sources
code: # Download BAM download.file(   "https://zenodo.org/records/19073177/files/P1_AI_possorted_genome_bam.bam",   destfile = "P1_AI_possorted_genome_bam.bam" )  # Download Seurat object download.file(   "https://zenodo.org/records/19072084/files/reference_list.rds",   destfile = "reference_list.rds" )  library(Seurat) seurat_obj <- readRDS("reference_list.rds")

title: External Data Sources
code: Wang Y (2026). FastQDesign Test Data. Zenodo. https://zenodo.org/records/19072084 https://zenodo.org/records/19073177

title: Experiment Design with FastQDesign
code: library(FastQDesign)  # Load power analysis data from literature df_power <- read.csv(system.file("data", "AIBM_power.csv", package = "FastQDesign"))  # Define experiment parameters budget <- 7500  # Total budget in USD power_threshold <- 0.7  # Statistical power target flowcell_capacities <- c(10^7, 5 * 10^7, 2 * 10^8)  # Reads per flowcell flowcell_costs <- c(1000, 2000, 3000)  # Cost per flowcell library_costs <- 5000  # Library preparation cost  # Run experiment design rst <- FastQDesign(   df_power = df_power,   budget = budget,   power_threshold = power_threshold,   flowcell_capacities = flowcell_capacities,   flowcell_costs = flowcell_costs,   library_costs = library_costs,   reads_valid_rate = 0.9 )

title: Experiment Design with FastQDesign
text: FastQDesign() function returns list containing: $p_ind: Individual sample design plot $p_share: Shared sample design plot $design: Design results table

title: Experiment Design with FastQDesign
text: Save generated plots:
code: # Save individual plot ggsave("design_individual.pdf", rst$p_ind, width = 8, height = 6)  # Save combined plot combined <- patchwork::wrap_plots(rst$p_ind, rst$p_share, ncol = 1) ggsave("design_combined.pdf", combined, width = 10, height = 10)

title: Using fastF Tool
text: fastF binary automatically installed FastQDesign package:
code: devtools::install_github("yuw444/FastQDesign")

title: Using fastF Tool
text: Extract cell barcode whitelist FastQ files: creates whitelist.txt output directory.
code: # Find whitelist from R1 FastQ file FastQDesign::fastF_freq(   R1 = "test_R1.fastq.gz",   out = "output_dir",   len = 16L )

title: Using fastF Tool
text: Filter FastQ files using whitelist downsampling:
code: FastQDesign::fastF_filter(   R1 = "test_R1.fastq.gz",   R2 = "test_R2.fastq.gz",   whitelist = "whitelist.txt",   out = "filtered_dir",   len = 16L,   seed = 42L,   rate = 0.1 )

title: Using fastF Tool
text: Summarize cell barcode UMI information:
code: FastQDesign::fastF_crb(   bam = "test.bam",   out = "output.tsv.gz" )

title: Using fastF Tool
text: Convert BAM sparse matrix format:
code: FastQDesign::fastF_bam2db(   bam = "test.bam",   feature = "genes.tsv",   barcode = "whitelist.txt",   out = "matrix_dir" )

title: Using fastF Tool
text: Extract custom tags BAM files:
code: FastQDesign::fastF_extract(   bam = "test.bam",   tag = "CB" )

title: Using fastF Tool
text: can also run fastF directly command line:
code: # Find whitelist fastF freq -R input_R1.fastq.gz -l 16 -o output_dir/  # Filter FastQ fastF filter -R input_R1.fastq.gz -r input_R2.fastq.gz -w whitelist.txt -o filtered/  # Extract CR/CB tags fastF crb -b input.bam -o output.tsv.gz

title: Sample Preparation and Analysis
text: Prepare reference data experiment design:
code: # Load reference data ref_list <- readRDS(system.file("data", "reference_list.rds", package = "FastQDesign"))  # Prepare sample (Seurat object) cb03_list <- SamplePrep(   ref_list[[1]],   use_default_res = TRUE,   cell_3d_embedding = FALSE,   interactive = FALSE,   min.pct = 0.2,   logfc.threshold = 0.3,   return.thresh = 0.05,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Sample Preparation and Analysis
text: Downsample cells simulate different sequencing depths:
code: # Downsample reference ds <- DownSample(   ref_list[[1]],   rate_cells = 0.1,   enable_PCR = TRUE )

title: Sample Preparation and Analysis
text: Perform pseudotime analysis using Monocle3: function returns list : - cds**:Monocle3CellDataSet−**cds**: Monocle3 CellDataSet - **pseudotime: Pseudotime values - $plot: Pseudotime visualization
code: # Perform pseudotime analysis pt_result <- FindPseudotime(   cb03_list,   root_cells = NULL,  # or specify root cell names   verbose = TRUE )

title: Sample Preparation and Analysis
text: Compare experimental samples reference:
code: # Compare samples match_result <- SampleMatch(   query = query_seurat,   reference = reference_list,   method = "seurat" )

text: Yu Wang. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Wang Y (2026). FastQDesign: scRNA-seq Experiment Design Tool. R package version 0.1.0, https://yuw444.github.io/FastQDesign/, https://github.com/yuw444/FastQDesign.
code: @Manual{,   title = {FastQDesign: scRNA-seq Experiment Design Tool},   author = {Yu Wang},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0, https://yuw444.github.io/FastQDesign/},   url = {https://github.com/yuw444/FastQDesign}, }

title: FastQDesign
text: goal FastQDesign guide investigator designing single-cell RNA sequencing(scRNA-seq) experiment. aim shift focus back raw FastQ reads Unique Molecular Identifier (UMI) matrix considering scRNA-seq experiment. paper now open-access, PMID: 40175506.

title: FastQDesign
text: can install development version FastQDesign GitHub :
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("yuw444/FastQDesign")

title: FastQDesign
text: fastF submodel FastQDesign framework, written C efficiency broad compatibility. helps generate pseudo-design datasets FastQ reference.

title: FastQDesign
text: basic example shows prepare subsamples reference comparison: example, aibm reference Seurat object, fastq_ds generated FastQ downsample fastF.
code: library(FastQDesign) library(Seurat)  aibm <- readRDS("~/Documents/Research/FastQDesign/reference_list.rds")[[1]]  ref_list <- SamplePrep(   aibm,   condition = "orig.ident",   cell_3d_embedding = TRUE,   interactive = TRUE,   min.pct = 0.2,   logfc.threshold = 0.3,   return.thresh = 0.05,   verbose = TRUE )  fastq_ds <- readRDS("~/Documents/Research/FastQDesign/bam_downsample_list.rds")[[1]]  ds_list <- SamplePrep(   fastq_ds,   n_clusters = 4,   condition = "orig.ident",   cell_3d_embedding = TRUE,   root_cells_ref = Cells(aibm)[aibm$root_cells],   min.pct = 0.2,   logfc.threshold = 0.3,   return.thresh = 0.05,   verbose = TRUE )  match_list <- SampleMatch(cb03_list, ds_list)

what: Generate One Dot in the Similarity Surface
title: FastQDesign
code: ## the similiarity from one subsample  ARI <- mclust::adjustedRandIndex(match_list$seurat_cluster.x, match_list$seurat_cluster.y)  JaccardCluster <- FastQDesign:::JaccardIndex(match_list$genes_cluster$ref, match_list$genes_cluster$ds) JaccardCondition <- FastQDesign:::JaccardIndex(match_list$genes_condition$ref, match_list$genes_condition$ds)  Kendall <- cor(match_list$cluster_match$pseudotime.x, match_list$cluster_match$pseudotime.y, method = "kendall")  similarity <- mean(c(ARI, JaccardCluster, JaccardCondition, Kendall))

title: FastQDesign
code: df_power_paper <- read.csv("~/Documents/Research/FastQDesign/AIBM_power.csv")  budget <- 7500 power_threshold <- 0.8 flow_capacities <- c(10^7, 5 * 10^7, 2 * 10^8) flow_costs <- c(1000, 2000, 3000) library_costs <- 5000  library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) rst_design <- FastQDesign(   df_power = df_power_paper %>% dplyr::select(n_cells, expected_reads_per_cell, power_fastq),   budget = budget,   power_threshold = power_threshold,   flow_capacities = flow_capacities,   flow_costs = flow_costs,   library_costs = library_costs )

title: FastQDesign — FastQDesign

title: FastQDesign — FastQDesign
code: FastQDesign(   df_power,   budget,   power_threshold,   reads_valid_rate,   flowcell_capacities,   flowcell_costs,   library_costs,   cell_increment = NA,   read_increment = NA )

title: FastQDesign — FastQDesign
text: df_power data frame power information, N(filtered cell number), cell numbers filtration data analysis(Seurat) pipeline R(reads required get per filtered cell), total number FastQ reads divide filtered cell numbers         total number may use fastF get sample power(0-1) defined weighted average Adjusted Rand Index matched cluster membership,       Jaccard Index cluster DE genes condition DE genes,       correlation index matched pseudotime(optional),       correlation index matched 3d cell embedding(optional) budget numeric budget power_threshold numeric power threshold reads_valid_rate percentage FastQ reads valid converted UMIs FastQ reference ./filtered_feature_bc_matrix/barcodes.tsv.gz alignment flowcell_capacities vector flow capacities flowcell_costs vector flow costs library_costs vector library costs cell_increment numeric cell increment design, default floor(max(df_power$N) / 50) * 5 read_increment numeric read increment design, default floor(max(df_power$R) / 100) * 10

title: FastQDesign — FastQDesign
text: list power information constraints ggplots

title: Convert BAM to SQLite UMI Matrix — fastF_bam2db
text: bam Path BAM file (required) feature Path feature list file (required) barcode Path barcode list file (required) Output directory (default: ".") dbname Database name (default: ":memory:") cell Rate cell barcode (default: 1.0) depth Rate depth (default: 1.0) seed Random seed (default: 926) umicopies Whether store umi.tsv.gz (default: FALSE)

title: Convert BAM to SQLite UMI Matrix — fastF_bam2db
text: Invisible NULL. Creates SQLite DB output directory.
